producer_note: Founded 1880 in Soave; Pieropan family farms 40 ha exclusively in Soave Classico, including Calvarino, La Rocca and Le Colombare crus. Organic.
Family winery founded 1874 with 60 ha entirely in Valpolicella Classica (San Pietro in Cariano, Fumane, Negrar). Organic-certified since 2015; Amarone pioneer.
Founded 2006 in Napa Valley by Italians Valentina Guolo-Migotto, Dario De Conti and Stefano Migotto; estate vineyards in Carneros and Wild Horse Valley. Napa Green certified.

Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe B; 11.3 ha planted 83% Merlot/17% Cab Franc on south-facing limestone. Owned by Gratiot Alphandery family; consultants Thienpont and Derenoncourt since 2002. Organic.
Historic estate (chateau 1612) bought 1989 by John and Veronique Cochran; 20 ha in Cotes de Bourg, certified Demeter biodynamic since 1989. Indigenous yeasts, unfiltered.
Family Margaux estate, owned by the Miailhe family since 1858, with vineyard records back to 1428. 25 ha in southern Margaux planted to Merlot, Cab Sauvignon, Petit Verdot, Cab Franc.
Founded 1996 by Errazuriz family (former Mondavi JV); 1,085 ha estate in Colchagua with 210 ha of vines (Cab, Carmenere, Syrah). 100% sustainable certified.
Founded 1978; 160 acres of estate vineyards across Napa with sites in Stags Leap District, Howell Mountain, Oakville, Rutherford and Carneros.
Family Saumur estate in Saint-Just-sur-Dive; 19.4 ha including 10.58 ha on the Breze hill. Romain Guiberteau took over 1996; organic, low-intervention.
Founded 1983 in Bruchsal by Ulrich and Marietta Klumpp; brothers Andreas and Markus run ~32 ha in Baden's Kraichgau, certified organic since 1996. Burgundian varieties.
Founded 2004 by Rafael Canizares; produces from old vineyards in Alicante, La Mancha, Jumilla and Valencia using Monastrell and Tempranillo. Organic.
Saint-Emilion Grand Cru Classe adjacent to Chateau Angelus; ~2.5 ha planted 100% Merlot. Owned by Pradel de Lavaux and de Bouard families; managed by Stephanie de Bouard-Rivoal.
Founded 2012 by Julien Brocard in Prehy near Chablis; biodynamic pioneer in Chablis (La Boissonneuse certified 2002). Seven single-vineyard cuvees.
Umbrian winery on the hills of Montefalco; 34 ha producing Sagrantino di Montefalco DOCG, Montefalco Rosso DOC and Trebbiano/Grechetto. Becca family owners since 2018.
Beaujolais natural-wine estate established by Louis Bertrand in the 1950s in the Grand-pre area straddling Fleurie and Morgon; carbonic maceration, unfined and unfiltered.
King Valley winery in Victoria's High Country at the foot of Mount Bellevue; cool-climate Pinot Noir, Shiraz and Prosecco. Acquired by Ross/Judd/Murtagh families 2018.
Founded 1993, subsidiary of Concha y Toro; vineyards across Casablanca, Colchagua, Bio-Bio and other Chilean valleys. Renowned for Pinot Noir (Ocio cuvee from Casablanca).
Founded 1883 by C.H. Wente in Livermore Valley; America's oldest continuously operated family-owned winery, ~3,000 acres of certified sustainable estate vineyards. Famous Wente Chardonnay clone.
Family estate Can Marti in Gelida (Alt Penedes) with 135 ha; producing Penedes still wines and Corpinnat sparkling since 1951 (vines since 1395). Certified organic 2016.
Founded 1870 in Bardolino by the Zeni family (5 generations); 60 ha across two estates in Bardolino and Valpolicella. Produces Bardolino, Lugana, Soave and Amarone.
Founded 1969; Schmidheiny family-owned since 1979. Estate vineyard of 125+ acres in Los Carneros, Napa, focused on Chardonnay and Pinot Noir.
Founded 1883 by Don Melchor de Santiago Concha y Toro; over 11,000 ha across Maipo, Rapel, Colchagua, Casablanca, Curico, Maule and Limari. Largest Latin American producer.
Founded 1924 by Jean Baptiste Audy; 4.6 ha on the Pomerol plateau planted 70% Merlot/30% Cab Franc. Owned by Bourotte-Audy family.
Saint-Emilion Grand Cru of 2.3 ha on the south plateau next to Belair-Monange; sold 2017 to Ets. Moueix and integrated with Belair-Monange from 2022 vintage.
Family winery in Costigliole d'Asti producing Barbera d'Asti, Moscato and other Piedmontese wines (note: Arione is also a Barolo cru in Serralunga d'Alba farmed by other producers).
Family-owned Marlborough estate established 1993 in Waihopai Valley; 400 acres planted to 8 varieties — Sauvignon Blanc, Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, aromatics. NZ Winery of the Year 2026.
Family house founded 1825 in Chalons-en-Champagne, the only Champagne house based there. Six generations; Gallo-Roman chalk cellars; flagship Cuvee Royale Brut.
Founded 1961 in Costigliole d'Asti by Rosangela Clerici Riccadonna; acquired ~2021 by Mura Mura (Grom founders). Focus on Barbera d'Asti and Piedmontese varieties.
Founded 1956 by Pedro Pavone Voglino in Isla de Maipo; family-owned with vineyards spanning the Maipo Valley from the Andes to the coast. Cab Sauvignon, Carmenere, Merlot.
Pernod Ricard label launched 1983 with Coonawarra Cabernet honouring Hugo Gramp; range sources from Coonawarra, Barossa Valley and Eden Valley. Cellar door in Barossa.
Pauillac Fourth Growth (1855), bought by Rothschilds (Lafite) in 1962; vines on the Milon hillside west of Lafite Rothschild on gravel/sand/limestone soils.
Chateauneuf-du-Pape estate dating to the 14th century; 25.3 ha (22.3 red, 3 white). Acquired 2006 by the Cazes family (Lynch-Bages).
Pauillac estate purchased by AXA group in 1987 from Billa family; 17 ha on gravelly soils planted 54% Merlot/45% Cab Sauvignon. Managed by AXA Millesimes.
Historic 105 ha Moulis-en-Medoc estate (records to 1560); Cru Bourgeois Exceptionnel. Owned by the Merlaut family since 1976; Cab Sauvignon-led blend.
Muller family estate at Wiltingen on the Saar since 1797; owns 8.3 ha of the Scharzhofberg vineyard. Legendary Riesling Auslese and TBA from the Saar.
Founded 2000 by Antonio Moretti Cuseri in Val di Noto, southeastern Sicily; 128 acres of vineyards focused on Nero d'Avola, plus Syrah and Grillo.
Founded 1895 in Courteron, Cote des Bar; first Champagne house to convert entirely to biodynamics (1989, certified 1992). 15 ha mostly Pinot Noir.
Founded 2001 by Doug and Darci Hill in Yountville; 120 acres of family-farmed Napa vineyards in Atlas Peak, Carneros and Oak Knoll.
Founded 1985 by Anne-Marie Girardin and Dominique Piguet, based in Auxey-Duresses; ~12 ha across 18 Cote de Beaune appellations from Pommard to Maranges.
Grower in Merfy on the Massif de Saint-Thierry, north of Reims; 14 ha across 13 parcels farmed organically/biodynamically by Alexandre Chartogne since 2006.
Founded 1970 by Henri Forner in Cenicero, Rioja Alta, with mentorship from Emile Peynaud. Vineyards in Rioja Alta and Rioja Alavesa; ~1,200 ha; family-led by Cristina Forner.
Family estate in Marlborough's Southern Valleys launched 2004 by ex-Cloudy Bay's Ivan Sutherland and James Healy; NZ's largest BioGro-certified organic vineyard.
Founded 1902 in Pedemonte, Valpolicella Classico; family-run with 135 ha across Valpolicella Classico DOC and Lugana. Fourth generation; 100% estate-owned vineyards.
Label launched 2016 by Arnaud Mortet (son of Denis Mortet); ~4 ha in Gevrey-Chambertin including Premiers Crus La Perriere and Lavaux St Jacques and Grands Crus Mazoyeres and Charmes-Chambertin.
Cooperative-based Champagne brand headquartered in Chouilly; Centre Vinicole groups 82 cooperatives and 5,000+ growers across 2,162 ha. Brand established 1976.
Bordeaux negociant brand producing 23 cuvees sourced across multiple appellations including Medoc, Bordeaux AOC and broader regional blends (L'Etre Magique, La Miraculeuse, La Mysteriale Medoc).
Family domaine in Beine, near Chablis, run by Bernard Michaut and family since 1950; 53 ha in Chablis on Kimmeridgian soils. IWSC French Wine Producer of the Year 2013.
Single-estate Jackson Family Wines property on Mount Veeder, Napa Valley, at 1,400-1,800 ft elevation. Cab Sauvignon, Merlot, Malbec, Cab Franc; winemaker Chris Carpenter.
Founded 2001 by David and Cheryl Clarke; 675 acres across 5 vineyards — 3 in Barossa Valley (Kabininge, St Kitts, Truro) and 2 in Eden Valley (Mount Crawford, Milton Park).
Founded 2009 by Kevin Judd (founding winemaker of Cloudy Bay) in Marlborough's Omaka Valley; Sauvignon Blanc, Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, Pinot Gris and Riesling using wild yeasts.
Family domaine founded 1930 in Cerseuil, Marne Valley; ~14 ha across 40+ plots in Cerseuil, Mareuil-le-Port, Troissy and Oeuilly. Pinot Meunier specialist; pioneer of single-vineyard cuvees.
Founded 1840; major Bordeaux negociant and proprietor with 500+ ha across Haut-Medoc, Pessac-Leognan, Saint-Emilion, Graves, Loupiac, Cadillac and Sauternes. Owns Chateau Belgrave.
Founded 1969 by Mario Fantinel; 300+ ha across Friuli DOC zones — Collio, Grave and Colli Orientali — plus Prosecco DOC. Sparkling, white and red wines.
Prosecco specialist (founded 1973) with vineyards across Veneto and Friuli plains and the Valdobbiadene hills (UNESCO site); seven Prosecco styles spanning DOC and Valdobbiadene Superiore DOCG.
Saint-Emilion Grand Cru Classe; 13 ha planted 80% Merlot/20% Cab Franc. Owned by the Comte de Neipperg family (also Canon-La-Gaffeliere).
Family Vayron estate in Pomerol since the 1840s; 9 ha next to Trotanoy planted 90% Merlot/10% Cab Franc on clay-iron and gravel soils. Frederique Vayron oversees winemaking.
Family winery founded 1996 by Nick and Andy Peay with winemaker Vanessa Wong; 53-acre estate in the far West Sonoma Coast, 4 miles from the Pacific. Pinot Noir, Syrah, Chardonnay; organic.
Founded 2004 by importer Jorge Ordonez and Alois Kracher; revives unfortified sweet and dry Moscatel from old mountainside vines (1902-1974) in the Axarquia. Sierras de Malaga DO.
Founded 1906 in Ay; 12 ha across 43 parcels (5 ha Grand Cru in Ay, 3 ha in Cote des Blancs, 4 ha Marne Valley). Pinot Noir and Chardonnay; sustainable viticulture.
Brand created 2005 by Prince Robert of Luxembourg (Domaine Clarence Dillon, Haut-Brion); blended Bordeaux red, white (from Pessac-Leognan, Graves, Entre-Deux-Mers) and Monbazillac sweet white.
Estate winery in Calistoga (Michael Graves architecture, 1987) with 450 acres across four Napa vineyards from Carneros to Calistoga. Chardonnay, Pinot Noir and Cabernet Sauvignon.
Tenuta Sette Ponti estate in coastal Maremma, Tuscany, purchased 1999 by Antonio Moretti; 45 ha (15 vineyard) at 330 ft. Cab Sauvignon, Sangiovese, Petit Verdot, Alicante, Vermentino.
Family Champagne house founded 1925 in Epernay; 4th generation. 5.5 ha mainly Premier Cru in the western Montagne de Reims, in organic conversion. Brut Nature/Extra Brut focus.
Founded 1987 by Vittorio Moretti in Adro, Franciacorta, in a former 1880 brick kiln; 160 ha and ~1.3M bottles/year of Franciacorta DOCG sparkling.
Margaux Fourth Growth (1855) in Cantenac; 77.5 ha planted 50% Cab Sauvignon/45% Merlot. Acquired by Alexis Lichine 1951; owned by Groupe Ballande since 1999. Vines in all 5 Margaux communes.
Historic Chateauneuf-du-Pape estate (chateau 1624); 25 ha CdP plus 18 ha Cotes-du-Rhone Villages across CdP, Orange and Sorgues. Certified organic, transitioning to biodynamic since 2022.
Family producer in Santo Stefano in the Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG zone; Follador family making sparkling since 1769. Cryomaceration of Glera grapes.
Tenuta del Pajaru in Manduria, Salento, run by 4th-generation Federico De Cerchio; old-vine Negroamaro and Primitivo grown organically with spontaneous fermentation.
Pauillac Fifth Growth (1855); acquired 2009 by Lorenzetti family who doubled vineyard to ~46 ha. Plantings 47% Cab Sauvignon/48% Merlot/3% Petit Verdot/2% Cab Franc.
Saint-Emilion Grand Cru Classe owned by the Nony family since 1934; 17 ha west of Saint-Emilion at the foot of the plateau. 78% Merlot, 19% Cab Franc, 3% Cab Sauvignon.
Founded 2005 by Dominique Moreau in Polisot, Cote des Bar; biodynamic 2.5 ha of mostly Pinot Noir. Single-vineyard, single-vintage, no-dosage Champagnes with native yeasts.
Family business founded 1986 by Franco Tommasi in an 18th-c. Venetian villa between San Pietro in Cariano and Fumane in Valpolicella Classica; 30 ha of Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara.
Established 1944 by the Lindner family in the Barossa; sources from across the Barossa GI including Lyndoch, Greenock, Ebenezer, Seppeltsfield and Eden Valley. Old Block Shiraz icon.
Precision Wine Co. founded 2010 in Napa by Trevor Sheehan as a negociant operation buying grapes/juice from individual vineyards across Napa Valley. Multi-label production (~250,000 cases/yr).
Founded 2006 in Rioja Alavesa by Jean Marc Sauboua, Roberto San Ildefonso and Bienvenido Munoz; vineyards near 700 m in the Sierra de Cantabria foothills. Tempranillo focus.
Chateau Haut-Bages Liberal, Pauillac Fifth Growth (1855); 30 ha next to Latour with limestone parcels. Run by Claire Villars-Lurton; certified organic 2019, biodynamic 2021.
Founded 1897 by Luigi Einaudi (later Italy's first President) in Dogliani; ~135 acres across Dogliani and Barolo (Bussia, Cannubi etc.) plus Barbaresco and Roero Arneis. Four generations.
Cooperative in Acerenza taken over by Valentino Sciotti (Farnese) in 2008; certified organic vineyards on volcanic soils of Mount Vulture for Aglianico del Vulture DOC/DOCG.
Famiglia Cotarella (formerly Falesco), founded 1979 by Renzo and Riccardo Cotarella in Montefiascone; 670 acres straddling Lazio and Umbria. Flagship Montiano Merlot.
Family domaine in Essoyes, Cote des Bar; 4 ha across three parcels (Fosse-Grely, Les Cognaux, Martin-Fontaine). Biodynamic certified 2014; ~22,000 bottles/yr by Benedicte and Manu Leroy.
Cantina Santa Barbara, founded by Stefano Antonucci in the hills of Jesi, Marche; 900,000 bottles/yr focused on Verdicchio dei Castelli di Jesi, Montepulciano and Lacrima di Morro d'Alba.
Label of NZ-born winemaker Chris Ringland; flagship Shiraz from 100-yr-old vines on Stone Chimney Creek property in the Eden Valley/Barossa Ranges; CR and other cuvees sourced across the Barossa.
Saint-Emilion Grand Cru garagiste; 4.6 ha planted 85% Merlot, 8% Cab Franc, 7% Cab Sauvignon. Owned by Alexandra and Nicolas Robin; consultant Stephane Derenoncourt.
Founded 1996 in Forino by brothers Antonello and Ciro Urciuolo; Irpinia (Avellino) producer of Fiano di Avellino, Greco di Tufo, Falanghina and Taurasi DOCG (100% Aglianico).
Founded 1998 by the Doix and Llagostera families in Poboleda, Priorat; ~20 ha of organic old-vine Garnacha and Carinena (some planted 1902). Cliff Lede 50% partnership since 2019.
Project of the Urciuolo family with vineyards scattered across Irpinia (Campania) and Puglia. Includes Taurasi, Irpinia Campi Taurasini and Irpinia Bianco (Fiano/Greco/Falanghina).
Founded 1957 by Gino Umani Ronchi; Bianchi-Bernetti family-owned with 210 ha — 110 ha Verdicchio Classico, 60 ha Rosso Conero and 30 ha in Abruzzo (Montepulciano d'Abruzzo).
Valerie Frison estate in Ville-sur-Arce, Cote des Bar (took over 2012 from Demarne Frison); 6 ha biodynamic-leaning, mainly Pinot Noir on Kimmeridgian clay-limestone. No dosage, minimal sulfur.
Premium Cab-Shiraz blend made from Barossa Valley fruit (vine age 27 yrs, 1503 GDD); 12-day ferment, 24 months in 100% new French and American oak.
Founded 1998 by the Canepa family (Hacienda El Condor in Peteroa from 1946). Sources from family-owned estates in Curico, Maipo and Maule Valleys; old-vine Cab Sauvignon (1940) and Malbec (1934).
Founded 1870 by Noe Apollonio in Salento, Puglia; ~120 ha of estate vineyards focused on indigenous Negroamaro, Primitivo, Malvasia Nera and Susumaniello. 'Impresa Storica d'Italia'.
Medoc estate in Ordonnac owned by the Delon family (Leoville Las Cases, Nenin); 60 ha planted 48% Merlot, 33% Cab Sauvignon, 18% Cab Franc, 1% Petit Verdot.
